violations.text: Taco chicken and beef was observed held at 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food had been received from Rogers high school less than 1 hour prior to the time of inspection and was reheated in the oven. Food items were observed reheated to 182 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The 2-door reach-in freezer located in the kitchen area was observed not holding frozen items solidly frozen and was not in good repair. Per PIC, unit stopped working properly last week and removed potentially hazardous food items from unit. Establishment is to repair, replace, or remove piece of equipment that is in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on 2-door reach-in #1. Refrigerator door gaskets shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to renew food safety manager with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

Chopped salad was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in the cafeteria cold line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, salad was loaded less than 20 mins prior to inspection. Salad was placed into freezer to rapidly cool prior to service. Salad was observed at 34 degrees Fahrenheit before returning to service from freezer. PIC observed that cold line was not turned on prior to service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

The external non-food contact surfaces of the dishwasher have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

The drain for the kitchen prep sink was observed to be draining slowly and overflowing onto the floor. Plumbing and drainage systems shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. The can opener was observed with an accumulation of food residue. Can opener was sent through dishwasher during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

Establishment is lacking an air gap at Food Prep Sink. 5-202.13 Backflow Prevention Air Gap An air gap between the water supply inlet and the flood level rim of the PLUMBING FIXTURE, EQUIPMENT, or nonFOOD EQUIPMENT shall be at least twice the diameter of the water supply inlet and may not be less than 25 mm (1 inch). New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2023
